title: Repository Repair, Release, and Collaboration Capstone · Repository Checkpoint
summary: Recover and ship a repository containing mixed work, a conflict, a mistaken commit, a remote update, and a release deadline. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Capture repository boundaries, config origins, current operation, worktree/index state, graph, remotes, and recovery refs before repair.
Order preservation, reproduction, local repair, tests, history cleanup, and publication from least to most irreversible.
Coordinate rewritten history, conflicts, ownership, release changes, and recovery instructions with explicit affected refs and people.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Repository Triage · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v5.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree. Competency probe: Capture repository boundaries, config origins, current operation, worktree/index state, graph, remotes, and recovery refs before repair.

Which action demonstrates Repository Triage without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for capture repository boundaries, config origins, current operation, worktree/index state, graph, remotes, and recovery refs before repair.

content.text: A quiz repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v5.0. steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Handoff Runbook: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz repository incident combines mixed work, divergent refs, one conflict, an incorrect public commit, and release v2.0. Preserve recovery refs, repair the least destructive layer, and verify graph plus tree. Competency probe: Publish an accessible repository runbook covering setup, workflow, gates, release, recovery, and escalation with tested commands.
steps.why: Retrieving Handoff Runbook through the concrete a complete Git foundations evidence portfolio case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Publish an accessible repository runbook covering setup, workflow, gates, release, recovery, and escalation with tested commands.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Experienced contributors do not need repository-specific recovery documentation.
